fields.title: Variables and Data Types
fields.theory_content:
Variables and Data Types in Data

What are Variables?

Variables are containers for storing data values.

Common Data Types

  • Numbers - Integers and decimals
  • Strings - Text data
  • Booleans - True/False values
  • Lists/Arrays - Collections of items
  • Dictionaries/Objects - Key-value pairs

Naming Conventions

Use descriptive names, start with lowercase, use underscores for multiple words.
fields.example_code:
# Simple Data Program
def main():
    # Get user input
    name = input("Enter your name: ")
    age = int(input("Enter your age: "))
    
    # Display output
    print(f"Hello {name}!")
    print(f"You are {age} years old")
    print(f"Next year you will be {age + 1}")

# Run the program
if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
fields.learning_objectives: By the end of this lesson, you will be able to understand and use variables concepts in Data.
fields.practice_exercises:
1. 🎯 **Prime Numbers**: Write a function to check if a number is prime
2. 📋 **List Operations**: Create a program that finds the largest and smallest numbers in a list
3. 🔁 **Factorial**: Write a recursive function to calculate factorial
4. 🔑 **Password Validator**: Create a function that validates password strength
5. 📈 **Fibonacci Sequence**: Generate the first N numbers in the Fibonacci sequence

fields.title: Control Flow: Conditionals and Loops
fields.theory_content:
Control Flow in Spring

Conditional Statements

if condition:
    # code executes if true
elif other_condition:
    # code executes if previous false and this true
else:
    # code executes if all false

Loops

# For loop - iterate over a sequence
for item in collection:
    process(item)

# While loop - repeat while condition is true
while condition:
    do_something()

Control Flow in Data

Conditional Statements

    # code executes if true
elif other_condition:
    # code executes if previous false and this true
else:
    # code executes if all false

Loops

# For loop - iterate over a sequence
for item in collection:
    process(item)

# While loop - repeat while condition is true
while condition:
    do_something()

Control flow statements make decisions and repeat actions.

    count += 1
